mentalModel.keyInsight: Using hash map to store value -> index mapping transforms O(n²) search into O(1) lookup by trading space for time
mentalModel.intuition: Instead of asking 'does this other number exist somewhere?', we ask 'have I seen the complement of this number before?' This mental shift from searching forward to remembering backward is the core breakthrough
mentalModel.analogy: Like a dance partner matching service - instead of checking every person to see if they're compatible with the current person, you remember everyone's preferences and instantly know if someone's ideal match has already arrived
mentalModel.commonMistake: Trying to use nested loops or sorting first, not realizing that hash map approach gives instant complement lookup while maintaining original indices

constraintIntuition.criticalConstraint: Needing to return actual indices, not just whether solution exists - this ruled out sorting approaches and made hash map natural
constraintIntuition.scalingLimit: Solution starts to struggle at around 10^7-10^8 elements due to memory constraints from hash map, but O(n) time complexity scales beautifully
constraintIntuition.alternativeApproach: If couldn't use extra space, would sort first then use two pointers, but would lose original indices and need extra work to recover them
constraintIntuition.constraintPattern: Classic 'space-time tradeoff' pattern where we accept O(n) space to achieve O(n) time

patternEvolution.patternFamily: Hash Map / Two Pointer - specifically the 'complement search' variant
patternEvolution.variation: Variation where we need indices preserved, so can't sort first like in standard two-pointer problems
patternEvolution.recognitionSignals: Combination of: finding pairs that sum to target + need original indices + no duplicates allowed
patternEvolution.evolutionInsight: Understanding evolved from thinking of this as 'searching' problem to 'remembering' problem - hash map isn't just storage, it's memory that enables instant recognition

similarProblems: Subarray Sum Equals K (running sum + hash map)
problemConnections.uniqueDifference: Two Sum finds exactly one pair with indices, while 3Sum finds all triplets, and Subarray Sum counts all valid subarrays
problemConnections.underlyingPrinciple: Using hash maps to transform existence questions into lookup operations - trading space for time complexity

futureApplication.recognitionSignal: Find pairs/complements + need indices/original order + target sum/difference
futureApplication.applicableTo: Any problem where you need to find elements that combine to meet criteria while preserving position information - currency exchange, matching algorithms, finding pairs in sequences
futureApplication.teachingMethod: Show the mental shift: instead of asking 'what goes with this?', ask 'have I seen what goes with this?' The hash map becomes your memory of previous encounters
